overview.label: Palette
overview.value: 21 stepped ramps — 18 chromatic families x 16 steps (100-1600); gray, transparent-white and transparent-black x 13 steps (25-1000)
overview.note: Plus 11 partial static-* ramps for mode-invariant colors, black/white and three avatar gradient stops: 372 tokens, every one marked private: true.

overview.label: Generation
overview.note: Leonardo — Adobe's open generator — is first-party documented for Spectrum 1's colors; no S2-labeled source repeats the claim and the token repo ships only literal rgb() values. Corroborated by measured values landing at WCAG thresholds (see contrast).

overview.label: Color space
overview.value: sRGB only — rgb()/rgba() in tokens, hex in compiled CSS
overview.note: Zero display-p3/oklch/lab/lch across all 2,469 dist tokens; the style macro's colorMix() mixes in sRGB. Authoring space unpublished for S2.

overview.label: Theming
overview.value: Build-time style macro -> atomic CSS; native CSS light-dark() + color-scheme
overview.note: No class or data-attribute switching; the Provider's colorScheme defaults to OS preferences. Forced-colors styles apply under @media (forced-colors: active).

overview.label: Naming
overview.note: accent-background-color-default, neutral-content-color-hover; palette reads {family}-{step}, component tokens {component}-{property}.

overview.label: Token scope
overview.value: Color, spacing, radius, typography, drop-shadow elevation, opacity
overview.note: No motion, breakpoint, cursor or z-index tokens anywhere in the 8-file source. 1,010 per-component dimension tokens live in layout-component.json.

layers.name: color-palette (global colors)
layers.note: Raw per-mode rgb() values; every token private: true — not meant for product code.

layers.name: semantic-color-palette + color-aliases
layers.note: Concept scales plus purpose-named aliases with state suffixes.

layers.name: color-component
layers.note: ~73 component-scoped tokens with an explicit component field.

ramps.name: static-blue
ramps.note: Mode-invariant: static colors keep one value across light, dark and wireframe.

tokenGroups.name: Semantic scales
tokenGroups.note: Whole palette ramps remapped onto concepts — accent, informative, negative, notice and positive are full 16-step alias scales (accent = blue in the shipped theme).

tokenGroups.name: Aliases
tokenGroups.note: Purpose-named tokens with state suffixes — the layer product code is steered to (the entire raw palette is marked private).

tokenGroups.name: Component tokens
tokenGroups.note: Component-scoped color (and opacity) tokens carrying an explicit component field in the source.

focus.label: Technique
focus.note: A shared focusRing() style-macro helper spread into every interactive component; matching focus-indicator-thickness / focus-indicator-gap tokens of 2px each.

focus.label: Color
focus.note: light rgb(75, 117, 255) / dark rgb(64, 105, 253); swaps to the OS Highlight keyword under forced-colors.

focus.label: Visibility
focus.note: outlineStyle is none by default and solid only under React Aria's isFocusVisible render prop.

contrast.label: neutral-content-color-default on background-base-color
contrast.note: Computed from shipped token values (observed, not official).

contrast.label: neutral-content-color-default on background-base-color
contrast.note: Computed from shipped token values (observed, not official).

contrast.label: accent-background-color-default vs white
contrast.note: Computed from shipped token values (observed, not official).

contrast.label: accent-background-color-default vs white
contrast.note: A hair above WCAG AA's 4.5:1 — characteristic of ratio-targeted generation, not hand-picking.

notes.text: Spectrum 1's gray system was deliberately built to recede — a Dieter Rams-inspired brutalism. Adobe judged it a liability at scale: grays felt washed out on complex multi-layer screens and unwelcoming to new users, which is the core motivation for S2's brighter, more expressive palette.

S2 planning began in late 2021 with 100+ team members across 12 themed working groups (Color and Data Visualization among them), targeting a portfolio of 100+ applications; the larger rollout landed in 2024.

Off-palette color is a hard boundary, not a negotiation: the Spectrum team rejects product designs that use colors from outside the system and steers teams back to tokens rather than absorbing one-off colors.

The S2 color direction wasn't invented from scratch — it was inherited from the 2019 'Spectrum for Adobe Express' theme (lighter, bolder, rounder, brighter), which became the literal starting point for the S2 palette.

S2 did two distinct things to color: increased contrast in the gray system, and rebuilt the chromatic system around Adobe's brand colors so product UI and brand could integrate — the named brand families are Orange, Celery, Cyan, Indigo, Fuchsia and Magenta.

Highlight color is treated as a scarce resource: S2 stripped extraneous accent color out of common controls to reserve it for high-attention, high-action moments — 'when everything is important, nothing is important.'

S2 icons ship as single SVGs driven by CSS color variables rather than baked-in fills — one asset flips between themes, takes custom colors, and supports gradients without duplication. The icon is a shape; color is injected at runtime.

Icons and design tokens are governed from the same production source of truth, explicitly to keep thousands of production assets, the tokens, and designers' daily mockups in parity — code, tokens and design can't drift.

Rather than fixed light/dark themes, S2 claims 'adaptive palettes' that generate accessible colors in real time, exposing user-facing Dark mode, Density and Contrast controls — contrast framed as built into the color system, not bolted on.

Color-vision-deficiency support drove concrete palette decisions, including a full overhaul of the data-visualization colors — one named criterion is avoiding 'color name conflicts' (peach vs orange vs salmon), a problem compounded across languages.

provenance.notes: AI-authored by reading the sources; predates the snapshot -> extract pipeline, so there is no committed snapshot and the values are not reproducible. The script-extractable part is ramps + tokenGroups, which mirror @adobe/spectrum-tokens variables.json (pinned 14.13.2) -- write an extractor for it before unarchiving this system. overview, layers, focus, notes and the measured contrast pairs are editorial or hand-measured and stay manual.
